title: Toffee Popcorn Braised Vegan Treat
description: A decadent herbal and crispy Canadian dish featuring a vegan twist with toffee popcorn braised in a rich sauce.

ingredients.name: 2 cups unsweetened almond milk
ingredients.notes: Use for soaking the pearl couscous.

ingredients.name: 2 cups of popped corn kernels (unsalted)
ingredients.notes: Ensure they are organic and free from additives.

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup pure maple syrup or agave nectar
2 tablespoons of soy sauce (or tamari for gluten-free)
1/4 cup coconut sugar (optional for extra sweetness)

instructions: Cook pearl couscous as directed on package, soaking it in unsweetened almond milk.
In a large skillet over medium heat, melt vegan butter and coconut oil together. Add popped corn kernels and cook until they start popping, stirring occasionally to prevent burning.
Gradually stir in the maple syrup, soy sauce (or tamari), and optional coconut sugar into the hot popcorn mixture. Let it come to a simmer for 2-3 minutes, stirring frequently.
Fold in ground cloves and vanilla extract. Season with salt to taste, then remove from heat. The mixture should be thick and caramelized.
Pour the warm corn mixture over cooked pearl couscous and toss gently until combined.
Transfer the dish onto a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Place it in the preheated oven for about 5-7 minutes or until the top is slightly crisp and golden brown.
